title: Lesson 273
transcript: you In this section we're going to talk about the three goals of security which everyone is aware of and sometimes referred to as the CIA triad. Although I think we're trying to get away from that terminology and really talk about meaningful words that describe what security should be addressing at all times in the organization in order to contribute and be aligned with goals and objectives. And so when we look at the three goals of security, again, let me frame this properly for you. Even though it says the goals of information security, It should more appropriately say the goals of security in general because how we apply confidentiality, integrity and availability should not just be limited to information. It should be done and applied to all valuable assets of the organization. and it's important for you to remember that as we go not only through this course but for the rest of your career because that's what security has to address today. The reason it says information security here is because this is directly taken from the exam outline that drives obviously what's important from the CISSP exam perspective, but if you think about it from an overall asset perspective, anything that has value to the organization needs to be protected from a confidentiality, integrity and availability perspective and you know what these address confidentiality is there to protect the sensitivity of the asset in fact that word is really good to use in relation to confidentiality. And when we look at integrity, we know that, and this is one that a lot of people don't understand or can relate to from a security perspective, but you as security professionals will know that integrity really means addressing the accuracy of assets, the meaningfulness, the timeliness, the currency of assets, because if those assets can be more current, more timely, more accurate, then it's gonna drive better business decisions. And that's why we need to apply security controls that are focused on integrity, because if you can make those assets more accurate, more timely, more current, then it's gonna drive better business decisions that are going to increase the value of the organization that's how we need to explain for example to people what these controls are all about and the availability one is pretty easy for all of us to understand we need to make sure that Certain systems, assets are always available to the organization whenever they are required. And in fact, instead of using the word availability, we should probably use the word criticality because that means much more. to our stakeholders that are interested in security doing the right things. And so when you look at confidentiality, integrity and availability, Maybe some of the better words to use in relation to those, first of all, is that they need to apply to all assets of the organization. But let's use words like sensitivity to reflect on confidentiality. Let's use, instead of integrity, we use words like timeliness, currency, accuracy and from an availability perspective, let's use words like criticality that mean much more to our stakeholders. Thank you.
